beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.title: Grendel's Characterization

scenes.title: Grendel hears the joy of Heorot
scenes.description: Grendel, dwelling in the moors as a cursed outcast (Cain's kin), endures torment hearing the nightly harp and creation-song from the lit hall. Defined as a theological exile unable to enter.
scenes.narrative_beat: Opening — establishes antagonist as spiritual exile; envy motivates violence

scenes.title: The first night raid
scenes.description: Grendel seizes thirty sleeping thanes, slaughters them, and carries bodies back to his lair. Dawn reveals emptied benches and grief in the hall.
scenes.narrative_beat: Middle — inciting catastrophe; converts threat to concrete loss

scenes.title: Twelve years of unrelenting reign
scenes.description: Attacks continue nightly for twelve winters with no truce or wergild. Heorot stands empty after dark—darkness against daylight rule.
scenes.narrative_beat: Closing — duration establishes scale of civilizational failure

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.dramatic_arc: Exclusion/envy → eruption → endurance (twelve years of grief)

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.title: Transition: Grendel's Terror to Beowulf's Summoning

scenes.title: Hrothgar's helpless mourning
scenes.description: Aged king sits sleepless and joyless; strongest counsels prove useless. Councils debate but find no answer.
scenes.narrative_beat: Opening — exhaustion of human law and counsel

scenes.title: Heathen vows at idol-shrines
scenes.description: In despair, Danes sacrifice at pagan temples, praying to the soul-slayer. Christian poet notes misplaced hope.
scenes.narrative_beat: Middle — spiritual nadir; all resources fail

scenes.title: News reaches Beowulf in Geatland
scenes.description: Word of Grendel crosses the sea. Beowulf, strongest man living, hears and resolves to seek Hrothgar.
scenes.narrative_beat: Closing — narrative pivot; focus shifts to hero's response

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.dramatic_arc: Helplessness → spiritual failure → the turn (hero hears and resolves)
music_arc_potential: Shift to resolute, ascending heroic motif

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.title: Beowulf's Voyage Across the Whale-Road

scenes.title: Choosing the company and launching
scenes.description: Beowulf selects fourteen warriors; guided by skilled seaman, leads them to prepared ship. Warriors bear bright war-gear.
scenes.narrative_beat: Opening — gathering will and means; hero answers un-summoned need

scenes.title: The voyage over the whale-road
scenes.description: Foamy-necked ship runs like a bird; sea crossed in good time as prow cuts swell. Open ocean imagery dominates.
scenes.narrative_beat: Middle — threshold-crossing between known world and danger

scenes.title: Sighting Danish cliffs and beaching
scenes.description: On day two, Geats sight gleaming sea-cliffs and disembark; warriors shake mail-shirts, give thanks for easy passage.
scenes.narrative_beat: Closing — arrival; journey resolves into landfall

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.dramatic_arc: Resolve and embarkation → whale-road passage → landfall on imperiled shore

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.title: Beowulf's Arrival in Denmark

scenes.title: The coast-guard challenges strangers
scenes.description: Hrothgar's watchman sees armed band, demands to know who they are and their right to land. Notes one appears a noble.
scenes.narrative_beat: Opening — law/order tests newcomers at the border

scenes.title: Beowulf's reply: lineage and errand
scenes.description: Beowulf answers courteously: Geats, Hygelac's companions, seeking to help Hrothgar overcome the secret destroyer.
scenes.narrative_beat: Middle — hero states identity, kin, and purpose; supplication in proper forms

scenes.title: Coast-guard grants passage
scenes.description: Watchman accepts them as friends, pledges the ship safe, leads them toward the gold-roofed hall.
scenes.narrative_beat: Closing — threshold opens; gatekeeper becomes escort

beowulf_fitts_2_5_analysis.dramatic_arc: Challenge at border → declaration and offered help → acceptance and guidance toward king

overarching_4fitt_arc: Kingdom's helplessness and the hero's summoning (Fitt 3)
Hero's arrival and formal greeting at the Danish threshold (Fitt 5)
synthesis.thematic_progression: From monster-alienation through civilizational failure, to hero-answered calling, to the re-establishment of lawful order and supplication
synthesis.music_composition_guidance: Consider a four-movement structure mirroring the four fitts: (I) dissonant, isolated monster-music; (II) exhausted, dirge-like civic collapse; (III) resolute, rhythmic seafaring march; (IV) formal, ceremonial threshold-crossing with hints of hope
